dialogues.text: You built rectangles with the same side length and wrote sentences about the areas. Now I want to see what you can do on your own. These three problems are for you to complete independently.
Here are three rectangles. One of them has an area that is a multiple of 3.
Your rectangle has 3 rows of 4 squares. The area is 12. That is a multiple of 3.
Here is a rectangle on the Hundred Grid. Width is 4. Height is 6. Area is 24.
Use the checked rectangle to fill the sentence.
Your sentence says 24 is a multiple of 4 because 4 times 6 equals 24. That's what the rectangle shows.
Build a rectangle that has a side length of 5 and an area of 30.
Your rectangle is 5 squares wide and 6 squares tall. The area is 30.
Now use the checked rectangle to fill the sentence.
30 is a multiple of 5 because 5 times 6 equals 30. You built it and you described it.
